original_abstract: In the field of digital image processing, a diverse range of methodologies exists to address tasks such as denoising, enhancement, segmentation, feature extraction, and classification. These techniques are essential for refining visual data, extracting critical information, and facilitating automated decision-making across various domains. While traditional methods rely on manually designed algorithms and heuristics, modern Deep Learning (DL) models offer the advantage of autonomous feature representation, capturing intricate patterns that conventional approaches often overlook. This review provides a comprehensive analysis of the shift from traditional to DL-based strategies. In image restoration and enhancement, specialized architectures like Denoising CNNs and RetinexDIP show significant potential in reducing noise and improving visual quality, though they face challenges related to parameter tuning and real-world complexity. Segmentation models, including U-Net and Mask-RCNN, demonstrate high precision in isolating objects but must contend with issues like overlapping entities. For feature extraction and classification, techniques ranging from standard CNNs to hybrid CNN-LSTM networks highlight the power of automated recognition, despite ongoing concerns regarding computational demands and model interpretability. By evaluating the strengths and limitations of these methodologies, this review offers a detailed understanding of the current state of the art, guiding practitioners in selecting the most effective models for practical applications in medical imaging, satellite analysis, and real-time processing.
humanized_abstract: Today there are a variety of methods applied in image processing for removing noise, image sharpening, segmentation, feature extraction and image classification. In fact all these methods are applied to enhance or to extract important information from the images and also for applying automatic decision in many fields. The old image processing methods always depend on manually designed algorithms and also by using empirical knowledge. But the Deep Learning (DL) methods can develop its own features of the data and this way can extract the hidden features that can not be extracted with old traditional methods. This paper is investigating the old traditional image processing methods as well as the new developing Deep Learning (DL) image processing methods. In the image restoration and enhancement techniques, Denoising CNN and RetinexDIP, the models that are developed for improving the image quality, by eliminating the noise, have applied successfully on several images but the parameters adjustment for all the models is causing difficulties. Also, the applied models do not give the best results in some complex real world images. For the image segmentation techniques the U-Net and Mask-RCNN are very successful with high accuracy, but also cause difficulties especially when some objects are overlapped with each other. For the feature extraction and image classification techniques, both the simple as well as hybrid models such as the standard CNN, hybrid CNN-LSTM have also give very promising results for the applied images, although with some drawbacks such as the large amount of computations time required from the models and also the lack of interpretation of the models developed. This paper also investigates on these downsides of the models. In all these applications of image processing methods, this paper also have investigated also the applications of the image processing methods on medical images, also on the satellite images and finally on the real time images.
